items.title: Jillaroos, jackaroos and an iconic event: The Brunette Races in pictures

children.content: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

articles.title: Young NT cattle ringers conquer outback obstacle course

children.content: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

image.altText: Man celebrates winning a competition while doing a lap of honour on a desert horse racing tack

items.title: NT Police says public has no need to be concerned about new officer class

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

image.altText: Police officers in uniform converse.

items.title: Thousands watch on as boats made of beer cans race on Darwin beach

children.content: Competitors at the annual Beer Can Regatta race rafts engineered from drinking containers, with beer or soft-drink cans the popular choice this year.

image.altText: Group of people watch a beer can boat race at the beach

items.title: The artefacts that tell the story of one of Australia's most notorious cases

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

gallery.altText: A museum curator wearing blue surgical gloves, holding a box which has a baby girl's pink outfit laid flat inside.
A small, dirty knit jacket laid flat on tissue paper.
A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

thumbnail.altText: A museum curator wearing blue surgical gloves, holding a box which has a baby girl's pink outfit laid flat inside.
A small, dirty knit jacket laid flat on tissue paper.
A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

items.title: Darwin vacation care worker charged with indecently touching child

children.content: NT Police allege the male educator, 40, indecently touched a child under the age of 16 at a vacation care program in Darwin.

image.altText: An NT Police Car parked on the street.

items.title: A speeding shadow minister puts the brakes on NT Labor’s rebuild

children.content: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: One of the deadliest cancers in Australia is becoming more common

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

gallery.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

items.title: 'I've got all the symptoms': Matty Robbins was a hero of the AFL. Now he's suffering

children.content: More than 30 professional and amateur Aussie Rules players have been diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis is striking all levels of the game.

articles.title: Randall says concussion left her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW

children.content: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

image.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

items.title: 'Terrible tragedy': Minister won't request coronial inquest into baby's death

children.content: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

image.altText: A baby sitting in hospital bed looking at camera

items.title: Alleged Russian spies charged with new espionage offence

children.content: Crown prosecutors presented a new bench charge for the couple of conspiracy to commit espionage.

image.altText: A composite image of the woman and man in seperate images.

items.title: NASA to bump telescope back into space to avoid it falling to Earth

children.content: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

items.title: 'Unimaginable pain': Brother tells of family's grief after doctor's death

children.content: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

image.altText: Artyom Avetisyan wears a suit and tie and holds a stethoscope while standing in front of a wooden wall.

items.title: Woman feared 'being sent to hell' if she escaped cult, court told

items.title: Lauryn Hill credited with reshaping music and inspiring generations

items.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of global fitness brand, dies aged 91

items.title: Ben Stokes's retirement a perfect example of his main character energy

children.content: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

children.content: The Victorian premier has backed her government's response to corruption in the construction industry, despite criticism from across politics and integrity experts.

items.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as record heatwave broken by severe storms

items.title: US and Iran to halt strikes as talks set to continue

children.content: The US and Iran will stand down on strikes and vessels will be allowed to move freely, as technical talks are slated to continue on all areas of the memorandum of understanding.

image.altText: A ship in the forground and another moving a long the horizon the background.

items.title: Renewed Gulf hostilities put markets on edge, ASX inches up

children.content: Local stocks begin the week higher as the end of financial year approaches. Wall Street was weighed down by another bout of selling in the tech sector. Follow market updates plus business news and analysis at our live blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Expanded World Cup called 'vulgar and ordinary' by Ghana coach. Is that fair?

children.content: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA

items.title: Crisafulli confirms children in state care have slept in government offices

children.content: David Crisafulli was briefed after the Together Union acknowledged over the weekend it was aware of reports of children sleeping in child safety offices.

image.altText: david crisafulli in a suit

items.title: Baby, young boys pulled alive from Venezuela earthquake rubble

children.content: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

items.title: Williams takes aim at anti-doping measures ahead of Wimbledon return

children.content: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

image.altText: Serena Williams walks to the training grounds at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club in London.

items.title: Power tools used in 'stupid' and potentially lethal acts of Stobie pole vandalism

children.content: Members of the public were put at "severe risk" of electrocution after four Stobie poles were vandalised in Adelaide's north in the past month, causing some to fall and expose high voltage electrical lines.

image.altText: A damaged Stobie pole lies on the ground near a popular walking and mountain bike track in the Cobbler Creek Recreation Park

items.title: 'Unlucky being born as Iranian': Diaspora's social fabric under strain

children.content: Iranian Australians are grappling with mistrust, harassment and isolation within a community deeply traumatised by an oppressive regime.
